     1	"""
     2	Avatar Task Service
     3	
     4	Multi-task AI service using avatar-engine. Supports any registered task type
     5	with unified interface: AI execution -> fallback -> error.
     6	
     7	Thread-safe: a single lock covers engine lifecycle + chat_sync() calls.
     8	Concurrent requests with different task types are serialized.
     9	"""
    10	
    11	import json
    12	import logging
    13	import re
    14	import threading
    15	import time
    16	from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
    17	
    18	from .cache import AICache
    19	from .config import AvatarConfig, load_avatar_config
    20	from .tasks.base import AITask, TaskResult
    21	from .tasks.registry import TaskRegistry, get_default_registry
    22	
    23	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    24	
    25	
    26	def _extract_json(text: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    27	    """
    28	    Extract JSON from AI response text.
    29	
    30	    Handles various formats: plain JSON, markdown code fences,
    31	    text before/after JSON.
    32	
    33	    Args:
    34	        text: Raw text response from AI provider
    35	
    36	    Returns:
    37	        Parsed dictionary
    38	
    39	    Raises:
    40	        json.JSONDecodeError: If no valid JSON found
    41	    """
    42	    text = text.strip()
    43	
    44	    # Strategy 1: Direct parse
    45	    try:
    46	        return json.loads(text)
    47	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
    48	        pass
    49	
    50	    # Strategy 2: Extract from markdown code fences
    51	    if "```" in text:
    52	        fence_pattern = r"```(?:json|JSON)?\s*\n?(.*?)\n?```"
    53	        matches = re.findall(fence_pattern, text, re.DOTALL)
    54	        for match in matches:
    55	            try:
    56	                return json.loads(match.strip())
    57	            except json.JSONDecodeError:
    58	                continue
    59	
    60	    # Strategy 3: Find JSON object by braces
    61	    first_brace = text.find("{")
    62	    if first_brace != -1:
    63	        depth = 0
    64	        in_string = False
    65	        escape = False
    66	        for i, char in enumerate(text[first_brace:], start=first_brace):
    67	            if escape:
    68	                escape = False
    69	                continue
    70	            if char == "\\":
    71	                escape = True
    72	                continue
    73	            if char == '"' and not escape:
    74	                in_string = not in_string
    75	                continue
    76	            if in_string:
    77	                continue
    78	            if char == "{":
    79	                depth += 1
    80	            elif char == "}":
    81	                depth -= 1
    82	                if depth == 0:
    83	                    json_str = text[first_brace : i + 1]
    84	                    try:
    85	                        return json.loads(json_str)
    86	                    except json.JSONDecodeError:
    87	                        break
    88	
    89	    preview = text[:200] + "..." if len(text) > 200 else text
    90	    raise json.JSONDecodeError(
    91	        f"No valid JSON found in response. Preview: {preview}",
    92	        text,
    93	        0,
    94	    )
    95	
    96	
    97	class AvatarTaskService:
    98	    """
    99	    Multi-task AI service using avatar-engine.
   100	
   101	    Supports any registered task type with a unified interface:
   102	    1. Cache check
   103	    2. AI execution via avatar-engine with task-specific system prompt
   104	    3. Fallback to semi-automatic (rule-based) if AI fails
   105	    4. Error result if all paths fail
   106	
   107	    Thread-safe: a single lock covers engine + chat_sync().
   108	    """
   109	
   110	    def __init__(
   111	        self,
   112	        config: Optional[AvatarConfig] = None,
   113	        registry: Optional[TaskRegistry] = None,
   114	    ):
   115	        self.config = config or load_avatar_config()
   116	        self.registry = registry or get_default_registry()
   117	        ext = self.config.extraction
   118	        self.cache = AICache(
   119	            cache_dir=ext.cache_directory,
   120	            ttl_days=ext.cache_ttl_days,
   121	        )
   122	        self._engine = None
   123	        self._engine_lock = threading.Lock()
   124	        self._current_task_type: Optional[str] = None
   125	
   126	    @property
   127	    def _provider(self) -> str:
   128	        """Active provider name."""
   129	        return self.config.provider
   130	
   131	    @property
   132	    def _model(self) -> str:
   133	        """Active model name from provider config."""
   134	        prov = self.config.providers.get(self.config.provider)
   135	        return prov.model if prov else ""
   136	
   137	    def execute_task(
   138	        self,
   139	        task_type: str,
   140	        input_data: str,
   141	        use_cache: bool = True,
   142	    ) -> TaskResult:
   143	        """Execute any registered task type. Thread-safe.
   144	
   145	        Args:
   146	            task_type: Registered task type name
   147	            input_data: Raw input for the task
   148	            use_cache: Whether to check/store cache
   149	
   150	        Returns:
   151	            TaskResult with output or error
   152	        """
   153	        task = self.registry.get(task_type)
   154	        if task is None:
   155	            return TaskResult(success=False, error=f"Unknown task type: {task_type}")
   156	
   157	        if not input_data or not input_data.strip():
   158	            return TaskResult(success=False, error="Empty input")
   159	
   160	        model_name = self._model or "default"
   161	        cache_content = f"{task.get_cache_prefix()}:{self._provider}:{model_name}:{input_data}"
   162	        provider_label = f"avatar:{self._provider}"
   163	        ext = self.config.extraction
   164	
   165	        # 1. Cache check (outside lock -- read-only, thread-safe)
   166	        #    Cache stores already-parsed output (no parse_result on hit).
   167	        if use_cache and ext.cache_enabled:
   168	            cached = self.cache.get(cache_content)
   169	            if cached:
   170	                logger.debug(
   171	                    "[task-service] Cache hit for %s (age: %.1fd)",
   172	                    task_type,
   173	                    cached.age_days(),
   174	                )
   175	                output = self._enrich_output(cached.result, cached.provider_id)
   176	                return TaskResult(
   177	                    success=True,
   178	                    output=output,
   179	                    provider_id=cached.provider_id,
   180	                    model=cached.model,
   181	                    cached=True,
   182	                    execution_time_ms=cached.execution_time_ms,
   183	                )
   184	
   185	        # 2. AI execution (lock covers engine + chat_sync)
   186	        start_time = time.time()
   187	        last_error: Optional[Exception] = None
   188	
   189	        with self._engine_lock:
   190	            # Double-call race guard: re-check cache after acquiring lock
   191	            if use_cache and ext.cache_enabled:
   192	                cached = self.cache.get(cache_content)
   193	                if cached:
   194	                    output = self._enrich_output(cached.result, cached.provider_id)
   195	                    return TaskResult(
   196	                        success=True,
   197	                        output=output,
   198	                        provider_id=cached.provider_id,
   199	                        model=cached.model,
   200	                        cached=True,
   201	                        execution_time_ms=cached.execution_time_ms,
   202	                    )
   203	
   204	            try:
   205	                engine = self._ensure_engine_for_task(task)
   206	                response = engine.chat_sync(input_data)
   207	                execution_time_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)
   208	
   209	                if not response.success:
   210	                    raise RuntimeError(response.error or "Engine error")
   211	
   212	                raw_output = _extract_json(response.content)
   213	                parsed = task.parse_result(raw_output)
   214	
   215	                if not task.validate_output(parsed):
   216	                    raise ValueError("Output validation failed: empty or invalid result")
   217	
   218	                logger.info(
   219	                    "[task-service] %s completed in %.1fs via %s",
   220	                    task_type,
   221	                    execution_time_ms / 1000,
   222	                    provider_label,
   223	                )
   224	
   225	                # Cache clean data (without _ metadata)
   226	                if use_cache and ext.cache_enabled:
   227	                    self.cache.set(
   228	                        content=cache_content,
   229	                        result=parsed,
   230	                        provider_id=provider_label,
   231	                        model=model_name,
   232	                        execution_time_ms=execution_time_ms,
   233	                    )
   234	
   235	                # Add tracking metadata AFTER caching
   236	                output = self._enrich_output(parsed, provider_label)
   237	                return TaskResult(
   238	                    success=True,
   239	                    output=output,
   240	                    provider_id=provider_label,
   241	                    model=model_name,
   242	                    cached=False,
   243	                    execution_time_ms=execution_time_ms,
   244	                )
   245	
   246	            except Exception as e:
   247	                execution_time_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)
   248	                last_error = e
   249	                logger.warning("[task-service] %s AI failed: %s", task_type, e)
   250	
   251	        # 3. Fallback (outside lock -- doesn't need engine)
   252	        fallback = task.get_fallback()
   253	        if fallback:
   254	            logger.info("[task-service] %s falling back to semi-automatic", task_type)
   255	            try:
   256	                fb_result = fallback(input_data)
   257	                if fb_result.success and isinstance(fb_result.output, dict):
   258	                    fb_result.output = self._enrich_output(
   259	                        fb_result.output, fb_result.provider_id or "fallback"
   260	                    )
   261	                return fb_result
   262	            except Exception as fb_err:
   263	                logger.warning("[task-service] %s fallback failed: %s", task_type, fb_err)
   264	                return TaskResult(
   265	                    success=False,
   266	                    error=f"AI failed: {last_error}; Fallback failed: {fb_err}",
   267	                    provider_id=provider_label,
   268	                    execution_time_ms=execution_time_ms,
   269	                )
   270	
   271	        return TaskResult(
   272	            success=False,
   273	            error=str(last_error),
   274	            provider_id=provider_label,
   275	            execution_time_ms=execution_time_ms,
   276	        )
   277	
   278	    @staticmethod
   279	    def _enrich_output(output: Any, provider_id: str) -> Any:
   280	        """Add _extracted_by and _ai_fields tracking metadata to output.
   281	
   282	        Works on dict outputs only. Returns the enriched copy.
   283	        """
   284	        if not isinstance(output, dict):
   285	            return output
   286	        enriched = dict(output)
   287	        enriched["_extracted_by"] = provider_id
   288	        enriched["_ai_fields"] = [
   289	            k for k in enriched.keys() if not k.startswith("_")
   290	        ]
   291	        return enriched
   292	
   293	    def _ensure_engine_for_task(self, task: AITask):
   294	        """Get or restart engine for task. MUST be called under _engine_lock."""
   295	        if self._engine and self._current_task_type == task.task_type:
   296	            return self._engine
   297	
   298	        # Clean stop before restart
   299	        if self._engine:
   300	            try:
   301	                self._engine.stop_sync()
   302	            except Exception as e:
   303	                logger.warning("[task-service] Engine stop failed: %s", e)
   304	            self._engine = None
   305	            self._current_task_type = None
   306	
   307	        # Load skills + create engine
   308	        skills_content = self._load_skills(task.SKILL_NAMES)
   309	        system_prompt = task.build_system_prompt(skills_content)
   310	
   311	        try:
   312	            from avatar_engine import AvatarEngine
   313	        except ImportError:
   314	            raise ImportError(
   315	                "avatar-engine is not installed. "
   316	                "See docs/avatar/getting-started.md for installation instructions."
   317	            )
   318	
   319	        engine = AvatarEngine(
   320	            provider=self._provider,
   321	            model=self._model or None,
   322	            system_prompt=system_prompt,
   323	            timeout=120,
   324	            safety_instructions="unrestricted",
   325	        )
   326	        engine.start_sync()
   327	        self._engine = engine
   328	        self._current_task_type = task.task_type
   329	
   330	        logger.info(
   331	            "[task-service] Engine started for %s: provider=%s, model=%s",
   332	            task.task_type,
   333	            self._provider,
   334	            self._model or "default",
   335	        )
   336	
   337	        return self._engine
   338	
   339	    def _load_skills(self, skill_names: tuple) -> str:
   340	        """Load skill markdown files by name. Graceful on missing files."""

   343	
   344	        from .skills import load_skill
   345	
   346	        parts = []
   347	        for name in skill_names:
   348	            path = self.config.skills_dir / f"{name}.md"
   349	            if path.exists():
   350	                content = load_skill(path)
   351	                if content.strip():
   352	                    parts.append(content)
   353	            else:
   354	                logger.warning("[task-service] Skill file not found: %s", path)
   355	        return "\n\n---\n\n".join(parts)

 succeeded in 0ms:
   283	def try_mount_avatar_engine(app) -> bool:
   284	    """
   285	    Attempt to mount avatar-engine's WebSocket/REST API into the FastAPI app.
   286	
   287	    Returns True if mounted successfully, False if avatar-engine not available.
   288	    This is called from main.py during app initialization.
   289	    """
   290	    if not AVATAR_ENGINE_AVAILABLE:
   291	        logger.info("Avatar Engine not installed — skipping mount")
   292	        return False
   293	
   294	    if not check_avatar_engine_compat():
   295	        logger.warning("Avatar Engine version incompatible — skipping mount")
   296	        return False
   297	
   298	    try:
   299	        from avatar_engine.web import create_api_app as create_avatar_app
   300	
   301	        config = load_avatar_config()
   302	        if not config.enabled:
   303	            logger.info("Avatar Engine disabled in config — skipping mount")
   304	            return False
   305	
   306	        system_prompt = build_system_prompt(config)
   307	
   308	        # Only pass config_path if the file actually exists — avatar-engine
   309	        # raises FileNotFoundError for missing paths instead of using defaults.
   310	        config_file = config.config_path
   311	        avatar_app = create_avatar_app(
   312	            provider=config.provider,
   313	            config_path=str(config_file) if config_file and config_file.exists() else None,
   314	            system_prompt=system_prompt,
   315	            api_prefix="",
   316	        )
   317	        # Mount at /api/avatar — engine routes use empty prefix (api_prefix="")
   318	        # so they register as /sessions, /ws, etc. Starlette prepends the mount
   319	        # path → final URLs are /api/avatar/sessions, /api/avatar/ws, etc.
   320	        # Synapse's own avatar routes (/api/avatar/status, /config, /skills, /avatars)
   321	        # are registered via include_router() which takes precedence over sub-app mount.
   322	        app.mount("/api/avatar", avatar_app)
   323	
   324	        # Store manager reference on parent app so its lifespan can
   325	        # initialize the engine (mounted sub-app lifespans are not
   326	        # called automatically by Starlette).
   327	        app.state.avatar_manager = getattr(avatar_app.state, "manager", None)
   328	
   329	        logger.info("Avatar Engine mounted at /api/avatar")
   330	        return True
   331	
   332	    except Exception as e:
   333	        logger.warning("Failed to mount Avatar Engine: %s", e)
   334	        return False

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	"""
     2	Avatar Skills System — domain knowledge loader.
     3	
     4	Skills are markdown files with Synapse-specific knowledge that get appended
     5	to the system prompt. Built-in skills ship in config/avatar/skills/,
     6	custom skills go in ~/.synapse/avatar/custom-skills/.
     7	
     8	Custom skills can override built-in skills by using the same filename.
     9	"""
    10	
    11	import logging
    12	from pathlib import Path
    13	from typing import Any, Dict, List
    14	
    15	from .config import AvatarConfig
    16	
    17	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    18	
    19	# Maximum file size for a single skill (50 KB)
    20	MAX_SKILL_SIZE = 50 * 1024
    21	
    22	
    23	def load_skill(path: Path) -> str:
    24	    """
    25	    Read a single skill file and return its content.
    26	
    27	    Strips YAML frontmatter (--- delimited) if present.
    28	    Returns empty string for binary/unreadable files.
    29	    """
    30	    try:
    31	        content = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    32	    except (UnicodeDecodeError, OSError) as e:
    33	        logger.warning("Cannot read skill file %s: %s", path, e)
    34	        return ""
    35	
    36	    # Strip YAML frontmatter (--- ... ---)
    37	    if content.startswith("---"):
    38	        parts = content.split("---", 2)
    39	        if len(parts) >= 3:
    40	            content = parts[2].lstrip("\n")
    41	
    42	    return content
    43	
    44	
    45	def list_skills(config: AvatarConfig) -> Dict[str, List[Dict[str, Any]]]:
    46	    """
    47	    List available skills with metadata.
    48	
    49	    Returns {"builtin": [...], "custom": [...]}, each entry containing:
    50	      - name: filename without extension
    51	      - path: absolute path string
    52	      - size: file size in bytes
    53	      - category: "builtin" or "custom"
    54	    """
    55	    result: Dict[str, List[Dict[str, Any]]] = {"builtin": [], "custom": []}
    56	
    57	    for category, skills_dir in [
    58	        ("builtin", config.skills_dir),
    59	        ("custom", config.custom_skills_dir),
    60	    ]:
    61	        if skills_dir is None or not skills_dir.is_dir():
    62	            continue
    63	
    64	        for path in sorted(skills_dir.glob("*.md")):
    65	            try:
    66	                if not path.is_file():
    67	                    continue
    68	                size = path.stat().st_size
    69	            except OSError:
    70	                continue
    71	            result[category].append({
    72	                "name": path.stem,
    73	                "path": str(path),
    74	                "size": size,
    75	                "category": category,
    76	            })
    77	
    78	    return result
    79	
    80	
    81	def build_system_prompt(config: AvatarConfig) -> str:
    82	    """
    83	    Build complete system prompt: base_prompt + built-in skills + custom skills.
    84	
    85	    Skills are loaded alphabetically. Custom skills with the same filename
    86	    as a built-in skill override the built-in version.
    87	    Files larger than MAX_SKILL_SIZE are skipped with a warning.
    88	    """
    89	    parts = [config.system_prompt]
    90	
    91	    # Collect skills: built-in first, then custom overrides
    92	    skill_map: Dict[str, tuple[Path, str]] = {}  # name -> (path, category)
    93	
    94	    for category, skills_dir in [
    95	        ("builtin", config.skills_dir),
    96	        ("custom", config.custom_skills_dir),
    97	    ]:
    98	        if skills_dir is None or not skills_dir.is_dir():
    99	            continue
   100	
   101	        for path in sorted(skills_dir.glob("*.md")):
   102	            try:
   103	                if not path.is_file():
   104	                    continue
   105	                size = path.stat().st_size
   106	            except OSError:
   107	                continue
   108	            if size > MAX_SKILL_SIZE:
   109	                logger.warning(
   110	                    "Skipping skill %s (%d bytes > %d limit)",
   111	                    path.name, size, MAX_SKILL_SIZE,
   112	                )
   113	                continue
   114	
   115	            name = path.stem
   116	            if name in skill_map and category == "custom":
   117	                logger.info(
   118	                    "Custom skill '%s' overrides built-in", name,
   119	                )
   120	            skill_map[name] = (path, category)
   121	
   122	    # Append skills in alphabetical order
   123	    loaded_count = 0
   124	    if skill_map:
   125	        parts.append("\n---\n\n# Domain Knowledge\n")
   126	
   127	        for name in sorted(skill_map):
   128	            path, _category = skill_map[name]
   129	            content = load_skill(path)
   130	            if content.strip():
   131	                parts.append(f"## Skill: {name}\n\n{content.strip()}\n")
   132	                loaded_count += 1
   133	
   134	    builtin_count = sum(1 for _, cat in skill_map.values() if cat == "builtin")
   135	    custom_count = sum(1 for _, cat in skill_map.values() if cat == "custom")
   136	    logger.info(
   137	        "System prompt built: %d skills loaded (%d builtin, %d custom)",
   138	        loaded_count, builtin_count, custom_count,
   139	    )
   140	    return "\n".join(parts)

     1	"""
     2	Abstract Base Task
     3	
     4	Defines the interface that all AI tasks must implement.
     5	Each task type has:
     6	  - AI path: skill-based system prompt + structured JSON output
     7	  - Semi-automatic fallback: rule-based / heuristic alternative
     8	  - Manual path: GUI for manual input (handled by frontend)
     9	"""
    10	
    11	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
    12	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    13	from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, Optional, Tuple
    14	
    15	
    16	@dataclass
    17	class TaskResult:
    18	    """Result from an AI task execution."""
    19	
    20	    success: bool
    21	    output: Optional[Any] = None
    22	    error: Optional[str] = None
    23	    provider_id: str = ""
    24	    model: str = ""
    25	    cached: bool = False
    26	    execution_time_ms: int = 0
    27	    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    28	
    29	    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    30	        """Convert to dictionary."""
    31	        return {
    32	            "success": self.success,
    33	            "output": self.output,
    34	            "error": self.error,
    35	            "provider_id": self.provider_id,
    36	            "model": self.model,
    37	            "cached": self.cached,
    38	            "execution_time_ms": self.execution_time_ms,
    39	            "metadata": self.metadata,
    40	        }
    41	
    42	
    43	class AITask(ABC):
    44	    """Abstract base class for AI-powered tasks.
    45	
    46	    Each task defines:
    47	    - task_type: unique identifier (e.g., "parameter_extraction")
    48	    - SKILL_NAMES: tuple of skill markdown files to include as domain knowledge
    49	    - build_system_prompt(): combines task instructions with skills content
    50	    - parse_result(): parses raw AI output into task-specific format
    51	    - validate_output(): validates parsed output
    52	    - get_fallback(): optional semi-automatic alternative (rule-based, heuristic)
    53	    """
    54	
    55	    # Task identifier (e.g., "parameter_extraction")
    56	    task_type: str = ""
    57	
    58	    # Immutable tuple of skill markdown file names (without .md extension)
    59	    SKILL_NAMES: Tuple[str, ...] = ()
    60	
    61	    @abstractmethod
    62	    def build_system_prompt(self, skills_content: str) -> str:
    63	        """Build complete system prompt: task instructions + skills knowledge.
    64	
    65	        Args:
    66	            skills_content: Concatenated markdown from skill files
    67	
    68	        Returns:
    69	            Complete system prompt for the AI engine
    70	        """
    71	
    72	    @abstractmethod
    73	    def parse_result(self, raw_output: Dict[str, Any]) -> Any:
    74	        """Parse and validate the AI response.
    75	
    76	        Args:
    77	            raw_output: Raw JSON output from AI provider
    78	
    79	        Returns:
    80	            Task-specific parsed result
    81	        """
    82	
    83	    def validate_output(self, output: Any) -> bool:
    84	        """Validate task output.
    85	
    86	        Default implementation accepts any non-None output.
    87	
    88	        Args:
    89	            output: Parsed output to validate
    90	
    91	        Returns:
    92	            True if valid
    93	        """
    94	        return output is not None
    95	
    96	    def get_fallback(self) -> Optional[Callable[[str], "TaskResult"]]:
    97	        """Return fallback function, or None if no semi-automatic alternative.
    98	
    99	        The fallback receives the raw input string and returns a TaskResult.
   100	        """
   101	        return None
   102	
   103	    def get_cache_prefix(self) -> str:
   104	        """Return cache key prefix for this task type."""
   105	        return self.task_type

     1	"""
     2	Task Registry
     3	
     4	Instance-level registry for AI task types. Prevents test pollution
     5	by avoiding class-level singletons.
     6	"""
     7	
     8	import logging
     9	import threading
    10	from typing import Dict, List, Optional
    11	
    12	from .base import AITask
    13	
    14	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    15	
    16	
    17	class TaskRegistry:
    18	    """Registry of available AI task types.
    19	
    20	    Instance-level (not a class singleton) to allow isolated testing.
    21	    """
    22	
    23	    def __init__(self) -> None:
    24	        self._tasks: Dict[str, AITask] = {}
    25	
    26	    def register(self, task: AITask) -> None:
    27	        """Register a task type. Overwrites existing registration."""
    28	        if not task.task_type:
    29	            raise ValueError("Task must have a non-empty task_type")
    30	        self._tasks[task.task_type] = task
    31	        logger.debug("Registered task: %s", task.task_type)
    32	
    33	    def get(self, task_type: str) -> Optional[AITask]:
    34	        """Get task by type name, or None if not registered."""
    35	        return self._tasks.get(task_type)
    36	
    37	    def list_tasks(self) -> List[str]:
    38	        """List all registered task type names, sorted."""
    39	        return sorted(self._tasks.keys())
    40	
    41	    def reset(self) -> None:
    42	        """Clear all registrations. For testing only."""
    43	        self._tasks.clear()
    44	
    45	
    46	# Default global instance (lazy-populated, thread-safe init)
    47	_default_registry = TaskRegistry()
    48	_default_registry_lock = threading.Lock()
    49	_default_registry_initialized = False
    50	
    51	
    52	def get_default_registry() -> TaskRegistry:
    53	    """Get the default task registry (auto-discovers on first call).
    54	
    55	    Thread-safe: uses double-checked locking for one-time initialization.
    56	    """
    57	    global _default_registry_initialized
    58	    if not _default_registry_initialized:
    59	        with _default_registry_lock:
    60	            if not _default_registry_initialized:
    61	                from .model_tagging import ModelTaggingTask
    62	                from .parameter_extraction import ParameterExtractionTask
    63	                _default_registry.register(ParameterExtractionTask())
    64	                _default_registry.register(ModelTaggingTask())
    65	                _default_registry_initialized = True
    66	    return _default_registry

     1	"""
     2	Parameter Extraction Task
     3	
     4	Extracts generation parameters from model descriptions.
     5	"""
     6	
     7	from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, Optional
     8	
     9	from ..prompts.parameter_extraction import PARAMETER_EXTRACTION_PROMPT
    10	from .base import AITask, TaskResult
    11	
    12	
    13	class ParameterExtractionTask(AITask):
    14	    """
    15	    Task for extracting generation parameters from descriptions.
    16	
    17	    Uses Prompt V2 optimized for:
    18	    - snake_case key naming
    19	    - Flat structure (no unnecessary nesting)
    20	    - Numeric ranges as {min, max}
    21	    - No hallucinated base_model
    22	    """
    23	
    24	    task_type = "parameter_extraction"
    25	    SKILL_NAMES = ("generation-params",)
    26	
    27	    def build_system_prompt(self, skills_content: str) -> str:
    28	        """Build V2 extraction prompt with optional skills knowledge.
    29	
    30	        Args:
    31	            skills_content: Concatenated markdown from skill files
    32	
    33	        Returns:
    34	            Complete system prompt for parameter extraction
    35	        """
    36	        prompt = PARAMETER_EXTRACTION_PROMPT
    37	        if skills_content.strip():
    38	            prompt += f"\n\n---\n\n# Reference Knowledge\n\n{skills_content}"
    39	        return prompt
    40	
    41	    def parse_result(self, raw_output: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    42	        """Parse and validate extraction result.
    43	
    44	        Performs basic validation and normalization:
    45	        - Ensures output is a dict
    46	        - Removes any internal keys (starting with _)
    47	
    48	        Args:
    49	            raw_output: Raw JSON output from AI provider
    50	
    51	        Returns:
    52	            Cleaned parameter dictionary
    53	        """
    54	        if not isinstance(raw_output, dict):
    55	            return {}
    56	
    57	        # Remove internal keys
    58	        return {
    59	            k: v for k, v in raw_output.items() if not k.startswith("_")
    60	        }
    61	
    62	    def validate_output(self, output: Any) -> bool:
    63	        """Validate extraction output.
    64	
    65	        Args:
    66	            output: Parsed parameters
    67	
    68	        Returns:
    69	            True if output is a non-empty dict
    70	        """
    71	        return isinstance(output, dict) and len(output) > 0
    72	
    73	    def get_fallback(self) -> Optional[Callable[[str], TaskResult]]:
    74	        """Return rule-based regexp fallback for parameter extraction."""
    75	
    76	        def _fallback(description: str) -> TaskResult:
    77	            from ..providers.rule_based import RuleBasedProvider
    78	
    79	            provider = RuleBasedProvider()
    80	            result = provider.execute(description)
    81	            if result.success and result.output:
    82	                parsed = self.parse_result(result.output)
    83	                return TaskResult(
    84	                    success=True,
    85	                    output=parsed,
    86	                    provider_id="rule_based",
    87	                    model="regexp",
    88	                    execution_time_ms=result.execution_time_ms,
    89	                )
    90	            return TaskResult(
    91	                success=False,
    92	                error=result.error or "Rule-based extraction failed",
    93	                provider_id="rule_based",
    94	            )
    95	
    96	        return _fallback

     1	"""
     2	Model Tagging Task
     3	
     4	Extracts tags, categories and content descriptors from model descriptions.
     5	Used for automatic categorization during import.
     6	"""
     7	
     8	from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional
     9	
    10	from .base import AITask, TaskResult
    11	
    12	
    13	# Known categories for validation
    14	VALID_CATEGORIES = frozenset({
    15	    "anime", "photorealistic", "illustration", "cartoon", "3d",
    16	    "pixel-art", "sketch", "painting", "concept-art", "abstract",
    17	})
    18	
    19	VALID_CONTENT_TYPES = frozenset({
    20	    "character", "landscape", "portrait", "full-body", "vehicle",
    21	    "architecture", "animal", "food", "clothing", "weapon", "nsfw",
    22	})
    23	
    24	
    25	class ModelTaggingTask(AITask):
    26	    """
    27	    Task for extracting tags and categories from model descriptions.
    28	
    29	    Extracts:
    30	    - category: primary style category (anime, photorealistic, etc.)
    31	    - content_types: what the model generates (character, landscape, etc.)
    32	    - tags: free-form descriptive tags
    33	    - trigger_words: activation words for LoRAs
    34	    - base_model_hint: mentioned base model compatibility
    35	    """
    36	
    37	    task_type = "model_tagging"
    38	    SKILL_NAMES = ("generation-params",)
    39	
    40	    def build_system_prompt(self, skills_content: str) -> str:
    41	        """Build tagging prompt with optional skills knowledge."""
    42	        prompt = TAGGING_PROMPT
    43	        if skills_content.strip():
    44	            prompt += f"\n\n---\n\n# Reference Knowledge\n\n{skills_content}"
    45	        return prompt
    46	
    47	    def parse_result(self, raw_output: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    48	        """Parse and normalize tagging result.
    49	
    50	        Ensures:
    51	        - category is a known string or 'other'
    52	        - content_types is a list of strings
    53	        - tags is a list of strings
    54	        - trigger_words is a list of strings
    55	        """
    56	        if not isinstance(raw_output, dict):
    57	            return {}
    58	
    59	        result: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    60	
    61	        # Category: normalize to known value
    62	        cat = raw_output.get("category", "")
    63	        if isinstance(cat, str):
    64	            cat_lower = cat.lower().strip()
    65	            result["category"] = cat_lower if cat_lower in VALID_CATEGORIES else "other"
    66	
    67	        # Content types: ensure list of strings
    68	        ct = raw_output.get("content_types", [])
    69	        if isinstance(ct, list):
    70	            result["content_types"] = [
    71	                s.lower().strip() for s in ct
    72	                if isinstance(s, str) and s.strip()
    73	            ]
    74	        elif isinstance(ct, str):
    75	            result["content_types"] = [ct.lower().strip()]
    76	
    77	        # Tags: ensure list of strings
    78	        tags = raw_output.get("tags", [])
    79	        if isinstance(tags, list):
    80	            result["tags"] = [
    81	                s.strip() for s in tags
    82	                if isinstance(s, str) and s.strip()
    83	            ]
    84	        elif isinstance(tags, str):
    85	            result["tags"] = [t.strip() for t in tags.split(",") if t.strip()]
    86	
    87	        # Trigger words: ensure list of strings
    88	        tw = raw_output.get("trigger_words", [])
    89	        if isinstance(tw, list):
    90	            result["trigger_words"] = [
    91	                s.strip() for s in tw
    92	                if isinstance(s, str) and s.strip()
    93	            ]
    94	        elif isinstance(tw, str):
    95	            result["trigger_words"] = [t.strip() for t in tw.split(",") if t.strip()]
    96	
    97	        # Base model hint: optional string
    98	        bm = raw_output.get("base_model_hint", "")
    99	        if isinstance(bm, str) and bm.strip():
   100	            result["base_model_hint"] = bm.strip()
   101	
   102	        return result
   103	
   104	    def validate_output(self, output: Any) -> bool:
   105	        """Validate tagging output. Must have category or at least one tag."""
   106	        if not isinstance(output, dict):
   107	            return False
   108	        return bool(
   109	            output.get("category")
   110	            or output.get("tags")
   111	            or output.get("content_types")
   112	        )
   113	
   114	    def get_fallback(self) -> Optional[Callable[[str], TaskResult]]:
   115	        """Return keyword-matching fallback for tagging."""
   116	
   117	        def _fallback(description: str) -> TaskResult:
   118	            tags = _extract_tags_by_keywords(description)
   119	            if tags.get("category") or tags.get("tags"):
   120	                return TaskResult(
   121	                    success=True,
   122	                    output=tags,
   123	                    provider_id="rule_based",
   124	                    model="keyword_match",
   125	                )
   126	            return TaskResult(
   127	                success=False,
   128	                error="No tags could be extracted from description",
   129	                provider_id="rule_based",
   130	            )
   131	
   132	        return _fallback
   133	
   134	
   135	def _extract_tags_by_keywords(text: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   136	    """Simple keyword-based tag extraction."""
   137	    text_lower = text.lower()
   138	    result: Dict[str, Any] = {}
   139	
   140	    # Detect category (specific multi-word categories first, then general)
   141	    category_keywords = [
   142	        ("concept-art", ["concept art", "concept-art"]),
   143	        ("pixel-art", ["pixel art", "pixel-art", "8-bit", "16-bit"]),
   144	        ("3d", ["3d render", "3d model", "blender"]),
   145	        ("photorealistic", ["photorealistic", "realistic", "photo", "photography"]),
   146	        ("anime", ["anime", "manga", "waifu", "animagine"]),
   147	        ("cartoon", ["cartoon", "comic", "toon"]),
   148	        ("illustration", ["illustration", "illustrated", "artwork"]),
   149	        ("painting", ["painting", "oil paint", "watercolor"]),
   150	    ]
   151	    for cat, keywords in category_keywords:
   152	        if any(kw in text_lower for kw in keywords):
   153	            result["category"] = cat
   154	            break
   155	
   156	    # Detect content types
   157	    content_keywords = {
   158	        "character": ["character", "person", "figure"],
   159	        "landscape": ["landscape", "scenery", "background", "environment"],
   160	        "portrait": ["portrait", "face", "headshot"],
   161	        "full-body": ["full body", "full-body"],
   162	        "nsfw": ["nsfw", "adult", "explicit"],
   163	    }
   164	    content_types: List[str] = []
   165	    for ct, keywords in content_keywords.items():
   166	        if any(kw in text_lower for kw in keywords):
   167	            content_types.append(ct)
   168	    if content_types:
   169	        result["content_types"] = content_types
   170	
   171	    # Extract trigger words (simple pattern: "trigger word: X" or "trigger: X")
   172	    import re
   173	    tw_match = re.findall(
   174	        r"trigger\s*(?:word)?s?\s*[:=]\s*([^\n<]+)",
   175	        text_lower,
   176	    )
   177	    if tw_match:
   178	        trigger_words = []
   179	        for match in tw_match:
   180	            for word in match.split(","):
   181	                word = word.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
   182	                if word and len(word) < 50:
   183	                    trigger_words.append(word)
   184	        if trigger_words:
   185	            result["trigger_words"] = trigger_words
   186	
   187	    # Collect free-form tags from detected attributes
   188	    tags: List[str] = []
   189	    if result.get("category"):
   190	        tags.append(result["category"])
   191	    tags.extend(result.get("content_types", []))
   192	    if tags:
   193	        result["tags"] = tags
   194	
   195	    return result
   196	
   197	
   198	TAGGING_PROMPT = """# Model Tagging Task
   199	
   200	You are a model tagging engine for AI image generation models (Stable Diffusion, Flux, etc.).
   201	
   202	## Instructions
   203	
   204	Given a model description, extract tags and categorization metadata.
   205	
   206	## Output Format
   207	
   208	Return ONLY valid JSON with these fields:
   209	
   210	```json
   211	{
   212	  "category": "anime|photorealistic|illustration|cartoon|3d|pixel-art|sketch|painting|concept-art|abstract|other",
   213	  "content_types": ["character", "landscape", "portrait", ...],
   214	  "tags": ["free-form", "descriptive", "tags"],
   215	  "trigger_words": ["activation", "words"],
   216	  "base_model_hint": "SDXL|SD1.5|Flux|Pony|..."
   217	}
   218	```
   219	
   220	## Rules
   221	- category MUST be one of the listed values
   222	- content_types: what the model is designed to generate
   223	- tags: descriptive tags about style, quality, purpose
   224	- trigger_words: only if explicitly mentioned in the description
   225	- base_model_hint: only if a base model is clearly mentioned
   226	- Omit fields if no relevant information found (don't hallucinate)
   227	- Return ONLY JSON, no explanation
   228	"""

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	"""
     2	Rule-Based Fallback Provider
     3	
     4	Uses existing regexp-based parameter extraction as fallback.
     5	No external dependencies, works offline.
     6	
     7	Standalone implementation — does not depend on the legacy provider hierarchy.
     8	"""
     9	
    10	import logging
    11	import time
    12	from dataclasses import dataclass
    13	from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
    14	
    15	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    16	
    17	
    18	@dataclass
    19	class RuleBasedResult:
    20	    """Result from rule-based extraction."""
    21	
    22	    success: bool
    23	    output: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
    24	    error: Optional[str] = None
    25	    execution_time_ms: int = 0
    26	
    27	
    28	class RuleBasedProvider:
    29	    """
    30	    Rule-based fallback provider using regexp extraction.
    31	
    32	    Wraps the existing parameter_extractor.py implementation.
    33	    Always available, no external dependencies.
    34	    """
    35	
    36	    provider_id = "rule_based"
    37	    model = "regexp"
    38	
    39	    def execute(self, description: str) -> RuleBasedResult:
    40	        """
    41	        Execute rule-based extraction on a description.
    42	
    43	        Args:
    44	            description: The description to extract parameters from
    45	
    46	        Returns:
    47	            RuleBasedResult with extracted parameters
    48	        """
    49	        start_time = time.time()
    50	
    51	        try:
    52	            logger.info("[ai-service] Task: executing, Provider: rule_based (regexp)")
    53	
    54	            # Import here to avoid circular imports
    55	            from src.utils.parameter_extractor import extract_from_description
    56	
    57	            result = extract_from_description(description)
    58	            output = self._normalize_result(result)
    59	            execution_time_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)
    60	
    61	            logger.info(
    62	                f"[ai-service] Extracted {len(output)} parameters (rule-based)"
    63	            )
    64	
    65	            return RuleBasedResult(
    66	                success=True,
    67	                output=output,
    68	                execution_time_ms=execution_time_ms,
    69	            )
    70	
    71	        except Exception as e:
    72	            execution_time_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)
    73	            logger.error(f"[ai-service] Rule-based extraction failed: {e}")
    74	            return RuleBasedResult(
    75	                success=False,
    76	                error=str(e),
    77	                execution_time_ms=execution_time_ms,
    78	            )
    79	
    80	    def _normalize_result(self, result: Any) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    81	        """
    82	        Normalize extraction result to dict format.
    83	
    84	        Args:
    85	            result: ExtractionResult from parameter_extractor
    86	
    87	        Returns:
    88	            Normalized dictionary of parameters
    89	        """
    90	        # ExtractionResult has a .parameters dict
    91	        if hasattr(result, "parameters") and isinstance(result.parameters, dict):
    92	            return result.parameters
    93	
    94	        if isinstance(result, dict):
    95	            return result
    96	
    97	        # Last resort: convert to string dict
    98	        return {"raw": str(result)}

tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:136:    def test_dir_without_avatar_json_ignored(self, tmp_path):
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:137:        """Subdirectories without avatar.json are ignored."""
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:138:        avatars_dir = tmp_path / "avatars"
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:139:        avatars_dir.mkdir()
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:141:        # Dir without avatar.json
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:142:        (avatars_dir / "no-json-dir").mkdir()
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:144:        (avatars_dir / "some-file.txt").write_text("not a dir")
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:146:        result = _list_custom_avatars(avatars_dir)
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:150:        """If avatar.json has no 'name' field, use directory name."""
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:151:        avatars_dir = tmp_path / "avatars"
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:152:        avatars_dir.mkdir()
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:154:        avatar_dir = avatars_dir / "unnamed-avatar"
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:155:        avatar_dir.mkdir()
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:156:        (avatar_dir / "avatar.json").write_text(json.dumps({"description": "test"}))
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:158:        result = _list_custom_avatars(avatars_dir)
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:160:        assert result[0]["name"] == "unnamed-avatar"
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:162:    def test_oversized_avatar_json_skipped(self, tmp_path):
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:163:        """avatar.json larger than size guard is skipped."""
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:164:        avatars_dir = tmp_path / "avatars"
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:165:        avatars_dir.mkdir()
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:167:        big_avatar = avatars_dir / "big-avatar"
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:168:        big_avatar.mkdir()
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:170:        (big_avatar / "avatar.json").write_text("x" * (_MAX_AVATAR_JSON_SIZE + 1))
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:172:        result = _list_custom_avatars(avatars_dir)
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:177:        avatars_dir = tmp_path / "avatars"
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:178:        avatars_dir.mkdir()
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:180:        # Create a real dir with valid avatar
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:181:        real_dir = tmp_path / "real-avatar"
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:183:        (real_dir / "avatar.json").write_text(json.dumps({"name": "Real"}))
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:185:        # Create symlink in avatars dir
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:186:        symlink = avatars_dir / "linked-avatar"
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:192:        result = _list_custom_avatars(avatars_dir)
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:196:        """avatar.json with non-dict content (e.g. array) is skipped."""
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:197:        avatars_dir = tmp_path / "avatars"
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:198:        avatars_dir.mkdir()
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:200:        arr_avatar = avatars_dir / "array-avatar"
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:201:        arr_avatar.mkdir()
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:202:        (arr_avatar / "avatar.json").write_text(json.dumps(["not", "a", "dict"]))
tests/unit/avatar/test_avatar_settings.py:204:        result = _list_custom_avatars(avatars_dir)

plans/audits/codex-audit-6-ai-integration.md:851:1. **Dynamické modely** — Modely se mění rychle (Gemini je už na 3.1). Avatar Engine scrapuje dokumentační stránky providerů a vrací aktuální seznamy modelů. Model dropdown v Synapse se aktualizuje automaticky — hardcodované modely v YAML example jsou jen initial fallback, reálně se modely vždy načítají dynamicky z backendu.

plans/PLAN-Avatar-v1.2-Dynamic-Models.md:13:1. **Dynamické modely** — Modely se mění rychle (Gemini je už na 3.1). Avatar Engine scrapuje dokumentační stránky providerů a vrací aktuální seznamy modelů. Model dropdown v Synapse se aktualizuje automaticky — hardcodované modely v YAML example jsou jen initial fallback, reálně se modely vždy načítají dynamicky z backendu.

plans/PLAN-Phase-4.md:1744:1. **Multi-version import NEFUNGUJE** - Výběr více verzí vytvářel pouze jednu dependency místo N
plans/PLAN-Phase-4.md:1754:Uživatel vybral 3 verze v Import Wizard, ale v pack.json se vytvořila pouze JEDNA dependency. 
plans/PLAN-Phase-4.md:1755:Toto je hlavní feature importu - každá vybraná verze = jedna LORA dependency.
plans/PLAN-Phase-4.md:1759:Frontend (ImportWizardModal) → API (version_ids) → Store (????) → PackService (1 dependency)
plans/PLAN-Phase-4.md:1768:  - Nová logika: Pro KAŽDOU vybranou verzi vytvoří samostatnou dependency

plans/PLAN-Phase-4.md:1783:- `test_single_version_import_creates_single_dependency` - 1 verze = main_lora ID

plans/audits/codex-audit-4-workflow-wizard.md:56:I’ll audit the existing repo against the workflow wizard spec and write the findings as markdown. I’m starting by reading the plan and locating workflow-related code, then I’ll trace the frontend/backend paths before producing the report.

plans/audits/codex-audit-4-workflow-wizard.md:456:./README.md:52:Ask questions like "What LoRAs do I have for SDXL?", "Find orphan blobs", or "Import this model from Civitai". The AI has access to 21 MCP tools for store management, Civitai search, workflow analysis, and dependency resolution.

   254	// =============================================================================
   255	// Main Component

   257	
   258	export const AvatarSettings = forwardRef<AvatarSettingsHandle>(function AvatarSettings(_props, ref) {
   259	  const { t } = useTranslation()
   260	  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
   261	
   262	  // Dynamic models from avatar-engine (scraped from provider documentation pages)
   263	  const avatarCtx = useAvatar()
   264	  const dynamicProviders = avatarCtx?.dynamicProviders
   265	  const [skillsExpanded, setSkillsExpanded] = useState(false)
   266	  const [pickerOpen, setPickerOpen] = useState(false)
   267	  const [selectedAvatarId, setSelectedAvatarId] = useState(() => {
   268	    try { return localStorage.getItem(LS_SELECTED_AVATAR) || DEFAULT_AVATAR_ID } catch { return DEFAULT_AVATAR_ID }
   269	  })
   270	  const selectedAvatar = ALL_AVATARS.find(a => a.id === selectedAvatarId)
   271	  const thumbUrl = useAvatarThumb(selectedAvatar, '/avatars')
   272	
   273	  const { data: status } = useQuery<import('../../../lib/avatar/api').AvatarStatus>({
   274	    queryKey: avatarKeys.status(),
   275	    queryFn: getAvatarStatus,
   276	    staleTime: 60_000,
   277	    retry: 1,
   278	  })
   279	
   280	  const { data: config, isLoading: isLoadingConfig, isError: isErrorConfig } = useQuery<AvatarConfig>({
   281	    queryKey: avatarKeys.config(),
   282	    queryFn: getAvatarConfig,
   283	    staleTime: 60_000,
   284	    retry: 1,
   285	  })
   286	
   287	  const { data: providers, isLoading: isLoadingProviders, isError: isErrorProviders } = useQuery<AvatarProviderType[]>({
   288	    queryKey: avatarKeys.providers(),
   289	    queryFn: getAvatarProviders,
   290	    staleTime: 60_000,
   291	    retry: 1,
   292	  })
   293	
   294	  const { data: avatars } = useQuery<AvatarAvatars>({
   295	    queryKey: avatarKeys.avatars(),
   296	    queryFn: getAvatarAvatars,
   297	    staleTime: 60_000,
   298	    retry: 1,
   299	  })
   300	
   301	  // Cache hooks
   302	  const { data: cacheStats } = useAICacheStats()
   303	  const clearCacheMutation = useClearAICache()
   304	  const cleanupCacheMutation = useCleanupAICache()
   305	
   306	  // Config mutation
   307	  const patchConfigMutation = useMutation({
   308	    mutationFn: patchAvatarConfig,
   309	    onSuccess: () => {
   310	      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: avatarKeys.all })
   311	    },
   312	  })
   313	
   314	  // No local changes to track — all mutations are immediate
   315	  useImperativeHandle(ref, () => ({
   316	    save: async () => {},
   317	    hasChanges: () => false,
   318	  }), [])
   319	
   320	  const isLoading = isLoadingConfig || isLoadingProviders
   321	  const isError = isErrorConfig || isErrorProviders
   322	
   323	  if (isLoading) {
   324	    return (
   325	      <div
   326	        className={clsx(
   327	          'rounded-2xl overflow-hidden',
   328	          'bg-gradient-to-br from-slate-dark to-slate-base',
   329	          'border border-slate-light/20',
   330	          'shadow-xl shadow-black/20',
   331	          'p-6'
   332	        )}
   333	      >
   334	        <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
   335	          <div className="w-12 h-12 rounded-xl bg-slate-light/20 animate-pulse" />
   336	          <div className="space-y-2 flex-1">
   337	            <div className="h-5 w-48 bg-slate-light/20 rounded animate-pulse" />
   338	            <div className="h-4 w-32 bg-slate-light/10 rounded animate-pulse" />
   339	          </div>
   340	        </div>
   341	      </div>
   342	    )
   343	  }
   344	
   345	  if (isError) {
   346	    return (
   347	      <div
   348	        className={clsx(
   349	          'rounded-2xl overflow-hidden',
   350	          'bg-gradient-to-br from-slate-dark to-slate-base',
   351	          'border border-slate-light/20',
   352	          'shadow-xl shadow-black/20',
   353	          'p-6'
   354	        )}
   355	      >
   356	        <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
   357	          <div
   358	            className={clsx(
   359	              'w-12 h-12 rounded-xl flex items-center justify-center',
   360	              'bg-red-500/10 border border-red-500/30'
   361	            )}
   362	          >
   363	            <AlertCircle className="w-6 h-6 text-red-400" />
   364	          </div>
   365	          <div>
   366	            <h2 className="text-lg font-bold text-text-primary">
   367	              {t('settingsAvatar.title')}
   368	            </h2>
   369	            <p className="text-sm text-red-400 mt-0.5">
   370	              {t('settingsAvatar.loadFailed')}
   371	            </p>
   372	          </div>
   373	        </div>
   374	      </div>
   375	    )
   376	  }
   377	
   378	  const aiEnabled = config?.enabled !== false
   379	  const engineVersion = status?.engine_version
   380	  const activeProvider = config?.provider
   381	  const skillsCount = config?.skills_count
   382	  const totalSkills = (skillsCount?.builtin || 0) + (skillsCount?.custom || 0)
   383	  const skills = config?.skills
   384	  const providerConfigs = config?.provider_configs || {}
   385	
   386	  // Build installed providers map
   387	  const installedMap = new Map<string, boolean>()
   388	  if (providers) {
   389	    for (const p of providers) {
   390	      installedMap.set(p.name, p.installed)
   391	    }
   392	  }
   393	
   394	  const handleToggleEnabled = () => {
   395	    patchConfigMutation.mutate({ enabled: !aiEnabled })
   396	  }
   397	
   398	  const handleSetDefaultProvider = (provName: string) => {
   399	    patchConfigMutation.mutate({ provider: provName })
   400	  }
   401	
   402	  const handleToggleProviderEnabled = (provName: string, currentEnabled: boolean) => {
   403	    patchConfigMutation.mutate({

   405	    })
   406	  }
   407	
   408	  const handleModelChange = (provName: string, model: string) => {

   410	      providers: { [provName]: { model } },
   411	    })
   412	  }
   413	
   414	  // Service default display string
   415	  const defaultProviderModel = activeProvider
   416	    ? `${PROVIDER_DISPLAY[activeProvider]?.displayName || activeProvider}${providerConfigs[activeProvider]?.model ? ` / ${providerConfigs[activeProvider].model}` : ''}`
   417	    : t('settingsAvatar.none')
   418	
   419	  return (
   420	    <div
   421	      className={clsx(
   422	        'rounded-2xl overflow-hidden',
   423	        'bg-gradient-to-br from-slate-dark to-slate-base',

   433	        )}
   434	      >
   435	        <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   436	          <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
   437	            <div className="w-[72px] h-[72px] rounded-full overflow-hidden border-2 border-purple-500/30 shadow-lg shadow-purple-500/20 bg-gradient-to-br from-purple-500/10 to-synapse/10 flex-shrink-0">
   438	              {thumbUrl ? (

   446	            <div>
   447	              <h2 className="text-xl font-bold text-text-primary">

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * AvatarProvider — Context provider wrapping the real @avatar-engine/react hooks.
     3	 *
     4	 * Calls useAvatarChat() ONCE at app root → single persistent WebSocket connection.
     5	 * All chat state (messages, connection, provider, permissions) flows down via context.
     6	 * Provides sendWithContext() that sends page context as structured metadata.
     7	 *
     8	 * Usage:
     9	 *   <AvatarProvider>
    10	 *     <LayoutInner />    ← uses useAvatar() to get chat + providers + compactRef
    11	 *   </AvatarProvider>
    12	 */
    13	
    14	import { createContext, useContext, useEffect, useMemo, useRef, useCallback, type ReactNode } from 'react'
    15	import {
    16	  useAvatarChat,
    17	  useAvailableProviders,
    18	  useDynamicModels,
    19	  useModelDiscoveryErrors,
    20	  AVATARS,
    21	} from '@avatar-engine/react'
    22	import type { UseAvatarChatReturn, AvatarConfig, ProviderConfig } from '@avatar-engine/react'
    23	import { usePageContextStore } from '../../stores/pageContextStore'
    24	import { buildContextPayload } from '../../lib/avatar/context'
    25	import { toast } from '../../stores/toastStore'
    26	
    27	/** Minimum backend avatar-engine version expected by this frontend. */
    28	const AE_MIN_VERSION = '1.0.0'
    29	
    30	/** Compare two semver strings (major.minor.patch). Returns true if a < b. */
    31	function semverLessThan(a: string, b: string): boolean {
    32	  const pa = a.split('.').map(Number)
    33	  const pb = b.split('.').map(Number)
    34	  for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
    35	    if ((pa[i] ?? 0) < (pb[i] ?? 0)) return true
    36	    if ((pa[i] ?? 0) > (pb[i] ?? 0)) return false
    37	  }
    38	  return false
    39	}
    40	
    41	/** Custom Synapse avatar with individual pose files (no sprite sheet). */
    42	const SYNAPSE_AVATAR: AvatarConfig = {
    43	  id: 'synapse',
    44	  name: 'Synapse',
    45	  poses: {
    46	    idle: 'idle.webp',
    47	    thinking: 'thinking.webp',
    48	    speaking: 'speaking.webp',
    49	  },
    50	  speakingFrames: 0,
    51	  speakingFps: 0,
    52	}
    53	
    54	/** All avatars: custom Synapse first, then built-in library avatars. */
    55	export const ALL_AVATARS: AvatarConfig[] = [SYNAPSE_AVATAR, ...AVATARS]
    56	
    57	interface AvatarContextValue {
    58	  chat: UseAvatarChatReturn
    59	  /** sendMessage with page context sent as structured metadata */
    60	  sendWithContext: (text: string) => void
    61	  providers: Set<string> | null
    62	  /** Dynamic provider configs with live model lists (scraped from provider docs) */
    63	  dynamicProviders: ProviderConfig[]
    64	  compactRef: React.MutableRefObject<(() => void) | null>
    65	}
    66	
    67	const AvatarContext = createContext<AvatarContextValue | null>(null)
    68	
    69	export function AvatarProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
    70	  const wsUrl = useMemo(() => {
    71	    const proto = location.protocol === 'https:' ? 'wss:' : 'ws:'
    72	    return `${proto}//${location.host}/api/avatar/ws`
    73	  }, [])
    74	
    75	  const chat = useAvatarChat(wsUrl, { apiBase: '/api/avatar' })
    76	  const providers = useAvailableProviders()
    77	  const dynamicProviders = useDynamicModels('/api/avatar')
    78	  const modelErrors = useModelDiscoveryErrors()
    79	  const compactRef = useRef<(() => void) | null>(null)
    80	
    81	  // Show warning toast when model discovery scraping fails for a provider
    82	  useEffect(() => {
    83	    for (const err of modelErrors) {
    84	      toast.warning(`Model discovery: ${err.message}`)
    85	    }
    86	  }, [modelErrors])
    87	
    88	  // Check backend avatar-engine status on first render
    89	  useEffect(() => {
    90	    fetch('/api/avatar/status')
    91	      .then(r => r.ok ? r.json() : null)
    92	      .then(data => {
    93	        if (!data) return
    94	        if (data.state === 'no_engine') {
    95	          toast.info('AI assistant unavailable — avatar-engine not installed')
    96	        } else if (data.state === 'no_provider') {
    97	          toast.info('AI assistant unavailable — no AI provider CLI found (gemini/claude/codex)')
    98	        } else if (data.state === 'setup_required') {
    99	          toast.info('AI assistant requires setup — install avatar-engine and a provider CLI')
   100	        } else if (data.state === 'incompatible') {
   101	          toast.warning(`AI engine v${data.engine_version} is incompatible — upgrade to v${data.engine_min_version}+ required`)
   102	        } else if (data.engine_version && data.engine_version !== 'unknown' && semverLessThan(data.engine_version, AE_MIN_VERSION)) {
   103	          toast.warning(`AI engine v${data.engine_version} is outdated — upgrade to v${AE_MIN_VERSION}+ recommended`)
   104	        }
   105	      })
   106	      .catch(() => { /* avatar status not available — non-critical */ })
   107	  }, [])
   108	
   109	  // Wrap sendMessage to attach page context as structured metadata
   110	  const sendWithContext = useCallback((text: string) => {
   111	    const { current, previous } = usePageContextStore.getState()
   112	    // Use current page context; fall back to previous only when current
   113	    // has no useful context (e.g. user is on the /avatar page itself)
   114	    const payload = buildContextPayload(current) ?? buildContextPayload(previous)
   115	    chat.sendMessage(text, undefined, payload ? { ...payload } : undefined)
   116	  }, [chat.sendMessage])
   117	
   118	  return (
   119	    <AvatarContext.Provider value={{ chat, sendWithContext, providers, dynamicProviders, compactRef }}>
   120	      {children}
   121	    </AvatarContext.Provider>
   122	  )
   123	}
   124	
   125	export function useAvatar() {
   126	  const ctx = useContext(AvatarContext)
   127	  if (!ctx) throw new Error('useAvatar must be used within AvatarProvider')
   128	  return ctx
   129	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * Layout — Main app shell with @avatar-engine/react AvatarWidget.
     3	 *
     4	 * AvatarWidget handles FAB / CompactChat / Fullscreen internally.
     5	 * The existing Synapse UI (Header, Sidebar, page content) is rendered
     6	 * via renderBackground — always visible behind all avatar modes.
     7	 *
     8	 * PermissionDialog (ACP) is a sibling OUTSIDE AvatarWidget.
     9	 */
    10	
    11	import { useEffect, type ReactNode } from 'react'
    12	import { useLocation } from 'react-router-dom'
    13	import { useQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'
    14	import {
    15	  AvatarWidget,
    16	  PermissionDialog,
    17	  StatusBar,
    18	  ChatPanel,
    19	  createProviders,
    20	} from '@avatar-engine/react'
    21	import type { EngineState } from '@avatar-engine/react'
    22	
    23	/**
    24	 * E2E test model override — when VITE_E2E_MODEL is set (via Playwright webServer),
    25	 * override the provider dropdown to show the cheap test model.
    26	 * The backend model is switched separately via run-e2e.sh.
    27	 */
    28	const E2E_PROVIDERS = import.meta.env.VITE_E2E_MODEL
    29	  ? createProviders({ gemini: { defaultModel: import.meta.env.VITE_E2E_MODEL } })
    30	  : undefined
    31	import { Header } from './Header'
    32	import { Sidebar } from './Sidebar'
    33	import { ToastContainer } from '../ui/Toast'
    34	import { AvatarProvider, useAvatar, ALL_AVATARS } from '../avatar/AvatarProvider'
    35	import { SuggestionChips } from '../avatar/SuggestionChips'
    36	import { usePageContextStore } from '../../stores/pageContextStore'
    37	import { getAvatarStatus, avatarKeys } from '../../lib/avatar/api'
    38	
    39	interface LayoutProps {
    40	  children: ReactNode
    41	}
    42	
    43	function LayoutInner({ children }: LayoutProps) {
    44	  const { chat, sendWithContext, providers, dynamicProviders, compactRef } = useAvatar()
    45	  const { pathname } = useLocation()
    46	
    47	  // Track page context for suggestions (Iterace 5)
    48	  useEffect(() => {
    49	    usePageContextStore.getState().setContext(pathname)
    50	  }, [pathname])
    51	
    52	  // Check if AI is enabled (master toggle)
    53	  const { data: avatarStatus } = useQuery({
    54	    queryKey: avatarKeys.status(),
    55	    queryFn: getAvatarStatus,
    56	    staleTime: 60_000,
    57	  })
    58	
    59	  const aiEnabled = avatarStatus?.enabled !== false
    60	
    61	  // When AI is disabled, render layout without AvatarWidget
    62	  if (!aiEnabled) {
    63	    return (
    64	      <div className="min-h-screen bg-obsidian flex flex-col">
    65	        <Header />
    66	        <div className="flex flex-1">
    67	          <Sidebar />
    68	          <main className="flex-1 p-6">{children}</main>
    69	        </div>
    70	        <ToastContainer />
    71	      </div>
    72	    )
    73	  }
    74	
    75	  return (
    76	    <>
    77	      {/* ACP Permission Dialog — OUTSIDE AvatarWidget (sibling) */}
    78	      <PermissionDialog
    79	        request={chat.permissionRequest}
    80	        onRespond={chat.sendPermissionResponse}
    81	      />
    82	
    83	      {/* AvatarWidget — handles FAB/Compact/Fullscreen INTERNALLY */}
    84	      <AvatarWidget
    85	        initialMode="fab"
    86	        customProviders={E2E_PROVIDERS ?? dynamicProviders}
    87	        messages={chat.messages}
    88	        sendMessage={sendWithContext}
    89	        stopResponse={chat.stopResponse}
    90	        isStreaming={chat.isStreaming}
    91	        connected={chat.connected}
    92	        wasConnected={chat.wasConnected}
    93	        initDetail={chat.initDetail}
    94	        error={chat.error}
    95	        diagnostic={chat.diagnostic}
    96	        provider={chat.provider}
    97	        model={chat.model}
    98	        version={chat.version}
    99	        engineState={chat.engineState}
   100	        thinkingSubject={chat.thinking.active ? chat.thinking.subject : ''}
   101	        toolName={chat.toolName}
   102	        pendingFiles={chat.pendingFiles}
   103	        uploading={chat.uploading}
   104	        uploadFile={chat.uploadFile}
   105	        removeFile={chat.removeFile}
   106	        switching={chat.switching}
   107	        activeOptions={chat.activeOptions}
   108	        availableProviders={providers}
   109	        switchProvider={chat.switchProvider}
   110	        newSession={chat.newSession}
   111	        onCompactModeRef={compactRef}
   112	        avatars={ALL_AVATARS}
   113	        avatarBasePath="/avatars"
   114	        renderBackground={() => (
   115	          <div className="min-h-screen bg-obsidian flex flex-col">
   116	            <Header />
   117	            <div className="flex flex-1">
   118	              <Sidebar />
   119	              <main className="flex-1 p-6">
   120	                {children}
   121	              </main>
   122	            </div>
   123	            <ToastContainer />
   124	          </div>
   125	        )}
   126	      >
   127	        {/* Fullscreen children — rendered when mode === 'fullscreen' */}
   128	        <div className="h-full flex flex-col overflow-hidden">
   129	          <StatusBar
   130	            connected={chat.connected}
   131	            provider={chat.provider}
   132	            model={chat.model}
   133	            version={chat.version}
   134	            cwd={chat.cwd}
   135	            engineState={chat.engineState as EngineState}
   136	            capabilities={chat.capabilities}
   137	            sessionId={chat.sessionId}
   138	            sessionTitle={chat.sessionTitle}
   139	            cost={chat.cost}
   140	            switching={chat.switching}
   141	            activeOptions={chat.activeOptions}
   142	            availableProviders={providers}
   143	            onSwitch={chat.switchProvider}
   144	            onResume={chat.resumeSession}
   145	            onNewSession={chat.newSession}
   146	            isStreaming={chat.isStreaming}
   147	            onCompactMode={() => compactRef.current?.()}
   148	          />
   149	          <main className="flex-1 flex flex-col min-h-0">
   150	            {chat.messages.length === 0 && (
   151	              <div className="px-4 pt-3">
   152	                <SuggestionChips onSelect={sendWithContext} />
   153	              </div>
   154	            )}
   155	            <ChatPanel
   156	              messages={chat.messages}
   157	              onSend={sendWithContext}
   158	              onStop={chat.stopResponse}
   159	              onClear={chat.clearHistory}
   160	              isStreaming={chat.isStreaming}
   161	              connected={chat.connected}
   162	              pendingFiles={chat.pendingFiles}
   163	              uploading={chat.uploading}
   164	              onUpload={chat.uploadFile}
   165	              onRemoveFile={chat.removeFile}
   166	            />
   167	          </main>
   168	        </div>
   169	      </AvatarWidget>
   170	    </>
   171	  )
   172	}
   173	
   174	export function Layout({ children }: LayoutProps) {
   175	  return (
   176	    <AvatarProvider>
   177	      <LayoutInner>{children}</LayoutInner>
   178	    </AvatarProvider>
   179	  )
   180	}

     1	/**
     2	 * AvatarSettings — Unified AI Settings Component
     3	 *
     4	 * Replaces both the deprecated AIServicesSettings and the old read-only AvatarSettings.
     5	 * Provides: master toggle, status overview, provider checkboxes with default-for-services
     6	 * radio, skills list, avatar picker, cache maintenance, and config file path.
     7	 */
     8	
     9	import { forwardRef, useImperativeHandle, useRef, useState, useEffect } from 'react'
    10	import { useQuery, useMutation, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'

    41	import { toast } from '../../../stores/toastStore'
    42	
    43	// =============================================================================
    44	// Types
    45	// =============================================================================
    46	
    47	export interface AvatarSettingsHandle {
    48	  save: () => Promise<void>
    49	  hasChanges: () => boolean
    50	}
    51	
    52	// Provider display info
    53	const PROVIDER_DISPLAY: Record<string, { icon: string; displayName: string }> = {
    54	  gemini: { icon: '✨', displayName: 'Gemini CLI' },
    55	  claude: { icon: '🤖', displayName: 'Claude Code' },
    56	  codex: { icon: '💻', displayName: 'Codex CLI' },
    57	}
    58	
    59	// =============================================================================
    60	// Stat Card
    61	// =============================================================================
    62	
    63	interface StatCardProps {
    64	  label: string
    65	  value: string | number
    66	  color?: 'synapse' | 'success' | 'warning' | 'danger' | 'neural'
    67	  icon?: React.ReactNode
    68	}
    69	
    70	const STAT_COLORS = {
    71	  synapse: 'text-synapse',
    72	  success: 'text-success',
    73	  warning: 'text-amber-400',
    74	  danger: 'text-red-400',
    75	  neural: 'text-neural',
    76	}
    77	
    78	function StatCard({ label, value, color = 'synapse', icon }: StatCardProps) {
    79	  return (
    80	    <div
    81	      className={clsx(
    82	        'px-4 py-3 rounded-xl',
    83	        'bg-slate-dark/30 border border-slate-light/20',
    84	        'flex items-center gap-3'
    85	      )}
    86	    >
    87	      {icon && (
    88	        <div className={clsx('w-8 h-8 rounded-lg flex items-center justify-center', 'bg-slate-mid/50')}>
    89	          {icon}
    90	        </div>
    91	      )}
    92	      <div>
    93	        <div className="text-xs text-text-muted">{label}</div>
    94	        <div className={clsx('text-lg font-bold', STAT_COLORS[color])}>{value}</div>
    95	      </div>
    96	    </div>
    97	  )
    98	}
    99	
   100	// =============================================================================
   101	// Model Select — custom themed dropdown with free-text input
   102	// =============================================================================
   103	
   104	interface ModelSelectProps {
   105	  value: string
   106	  models: string[]
   107	  placeholder: string
   108	  disabled?: boolean
   109	  onChange: (model: string) => void
   110	}
   111	
   112	function ModelSelect({ value, models, placeholder, disabled, onChange }: ModelSelectProps) {
   113	  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false)
   114	  const [editing, setEditing] = useState(false)
   115	  const [inputValue, setInputValue] = useState(value)
   116	  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
   117	  const inputRef = useRef<HTMLInputElement>(null)
   118	
   119	  // Sync input value when prop changes externally
   120	  useEffect(() => { setInputValue(value) }, [value])
   121	
   122	  // Close on outside click
   123	  useEffect(() => {
   124	    if (!open) return
   125	    const handler = (e: MouseEvent) => {
   126	      if (containerRef.current && !containerRef.current.contains(e.target as Node)) {
   127	        setOpen(false)
   128	        setEditing(false)
   129	      }
   130	    }
   131	    document.addEventListener('mousedown', handler)
   132	    return () => document.removeEventListener('mousedown', handler)
   133	  }, [open])
   134	
   135	  const handleSelect = (m: string) => {
   136	    onChange(m)
   137	    setInputValue(m)
   138	    setOpen(false)
   139	    setEditing(false)
   140	  }
   141	
   142	  const handleInputBlur = () => {
   143	    // Commit typed value on blur (small delay so click on option registers first)
   144	    setTimeout(() => {
   145	      if (inputValue !== value) onChange(inputValue)
   146	      setEditing(false)
   147	    }, 150)
   148	  }
   149	
   150	  const handleInputKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => {
   151	    if (e.key === 'Enter') {
   152	      e.preventDefault()
   153	      onChange(inputValue)
   154	      setOpen(false)
   155	      setEditing(false)
   156	      inputRef.current?.blur()
   157	    } else if (e.key === 'Escape') {
   158	      setInputValue(value)
   159	      setOpen(false)
   160	      setEditing(false)
   161	      inputRef.current?.blur()
   162	    }
   163	  }
   164	
   165	  return (
   166	    <div ref={containerRef} className="relative w-56">
   167	      {/* Display / Input */}
   168	      {editing ? (
   169	        <input
   170	          ref={inputRef}
   171	          type="text"
   172	          value={inputValue}
   173	          onChange={(e) => setInputValue(e.target.value)}
   174	          onBlur={handleInputBlur}
   175	          onKeyDown={handleInputKeyDown}
   176	          placeholder={placeholder}
   177	          disabled={disabled}
   178	          autoFocus
   179	          className={clsx(
   180	            'w-full px-3 py-1.5 pr-7 rounded-lg text-sm font-mono',
   181	            'bg-slate-dark/50 border border-purple-500/50',
   182	            'text-text-secondary placeholder:text-text-muted/40',
   183	            'focus:outline-none',
   184	            disabled && 'opacity-40 cursor-not-allowed'
   185	          )}
   186	        />
   187	      ) : (
   188	        <button
   189	          type="button"
   190	          onClick={() => {
   191	            if (disabled) return
   192	            setOpen(!open)
   193	          }}
   194	          onDoubleClick={() => {
   195	            if (disabled) return
   196	            setEditing(true)
   197	            setOpen(false)
   198	          }}
   199	          disabled={disabled}
   200	          className={clsx(
   201	            'w-full px-3 py-1.5 pr-7 rounded-lg text-sm font-mono text-left',
   202	            'bg-slate-dark/50 border border-slate-light/20',
   203	            'text-text-secondary',
   204	            disabled ? 'opacity-40 cursor-not-allowed' : 'hover:border-purple-500/30 cursor-pointer',
   205	            !value && 'text-text-muted/40'
   206	          )}
   207	        >
   208	          {value || placeholder}
   209	        </button>
   210	      )}
   211	
   212	      {/* Chevron */}
   213	      {!editing && !disabled && (
   214	        <ChevronDown className={clsx(
   215	          'absolute right-2 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 w-3.5 h-3.5 text-text-muted/50 pointer-events-none transition-transform',
   216	          open && 'rotate-180'
   217	        )} />
   218	      )}
   219	
   220	      {/* Dropdown */}
   221	      {open && !disabled && models.length > 0 && (
   222	        <div className={clsx(
   223	          'absolute z-50 mt-1 w-full py-1 rounded-lg',
   224	          'bg-slate-dark border border-slate-light/30',
   225	          'shadow-xl shadow-black/40',
   226	          'max-h-48 overflow-y-auto'
   227	        )}>
   228	          {models.map((m) => (
   229	            <button
   230	              key={m}
   231	              type="button"
   232	              onClick={() => handleSelect(m)}
   233	              className={clsx(
   234	                'w-full px-3 py-1.5 text-left text-sm font-mono',
   235	                'hover:bg-purple-500/15 transition-colors',
   236	                m === value
   237	                  ? 'text-purple-400 bg-purple-500/10'
   238	                  : 'text-text-secondary'
   239	              )}
   240	            >
